content:
First write \(n=[r]\) and \(x=\{r\}\), so \(r=n+x\) and \(0\le x<1\).  The equation is
\[
25x+n=125
\;\;\Longrightarrow\;\;
x=\frac{125-n}{25}.
\]
For \(0\le x<1\) we need
\[
0\le\frac{125-n}{25}<1
\;\Longrightarrow\;
100< n\le125,
\]
so \(n=101,102,\dots,125\).  For each such \(n\),
\[
r=n+x \;=\; n+\frac{125-n}{25}
\;=\;\frac{24n+125}{25}.
\]
Hence the sum of all solutions is
\[
\sum_{n=101}^{125}\Bigl(n+\frac{125-n}{25}\Bigr)
=\sum_{n=101}^{125}n \;+\;\frac1{25}\sum_{n=101}^{125}(125-n).
\]
There are \(25\) terms and
\[
\sum_{n=101}^{125}n=\frac{(101+125)\cdot25}{2}=2825,
\quad
\sum_{n=101}^{125}(125-n)=125\cdot25-2825=300.
\]
Therefore the total is
\[
2825+\frac{300}{25}=2825+12=2837.
\]
Answer: \(\boxed{2837}\).
